sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports feeling tired, lacking energy, and experiencing swollen, painful elbows. He also reports nausea and difficulty eating. He denies experiencing high fevers. The patient reports a rash resembling a bull's-eye on his abdomen.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals bilateral elbow swelling and tenderness to palpation. A bull's-eye rash is noted on the patient's abdomen, which is warm to the touch. Vital signs are stable. The remainder of the physical exam is unremarkable.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has newly diagnosed Lyme disease and type 2 diabetes. He is experiencing symptoms consistent with Lyme disease, including fatigue, joint pain, and nausea.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe doxycycline 100 milligrams twice daily for three weeks. Order a hemoglobin A1c test. Continue metformin as prescribed. Order a lipid panel. Schedule a follow-up appointment for one week. Order lisinopril 10 milligrams daily.
